task_completion.description: The agent successfully loaded the chest CT scan into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible with a chest CT volume loaded, showing axial, coronal, and sagittal views of the thorax.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the axial slice showing the carina (tracheal bifurcation).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial view displays the specific slice where the trachea splits into the left and right main bronchi (often looks like a figure-8 or an inverted 'V' shape in the airway).

task_completion.description: The agent placed an Angle markup with the vertex at the carina and arms along the bronchi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An angle measurement widget is visible on the slice. The vertex point is centered at the bifurcation, and the two endpoints extend down the centerlines of the left and right main bronchi.

task_completion.description: The measured angle value is visible and falls within the expected range for this dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The angle value is displayed (either on the view or in the Markups table) and reads approximately 70 to 73 degrees (allow 60-80 degrees visually). The value should not be extreme (<50 or >100).

task_completion.description: The agent generated a JSON report containing the angle, classification, and vertebral level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file preview shows 'carinal_report.json' content. It includes 'measured_angle_degrees' (approx 70-73), 'classification': 'Normal', and 'vertebral_level' (e.g., T5, T6, or T7).

integrity.description: The agent used the Slicer GUI to perform the measurement rather than scripting the result directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Slicer application window, the Markups module panel, or interaction with the slice views, rather than just a terminal window echoing text to a file.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the Angle tool in the Markups module.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Markups module is open in the sidebar, or the toolbar shows the Angle tool selected/active during the process.

integrity.description: The agent derived the value from the software, not from the prompt description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value in the report matches the value displayed in the Slicer interface (Markups table or view annotation) in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The carinal angle is formed at the tracheal bifurcation (typically T5-T7 vertebral level). In a normal adult chest CT, this angle is typically between 40-100 degrees. For the provided dataset, the expected angle is approximately 70-73 degrees. A measurement significantly outside the 60-80 degree range suggests incorrect anatomical placement.
